query: SPC High Risk Day 1 Convective Outlook historical frequency by month, especially late May. How many High Risk days per year on average, and how many fall in the May 17-30 window historically?
answer:
## SPC High Risk Day 1 Convective Outlook: Historical Frequency Research

### Overview

The Storm Prediction Center (SPC) High Risk Day 1 Convective Outlook is the highest categorical risk level issued by the SPC. It is rare, historically averaging about **3.6 per year** over the full 1982–2025 record (160 total High Risk days across 44 years). The frequency has declined sharply in recent decades.

---

### Annual Frequency by Era

| Era | High Risk Days | Years | Per Year Avg |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1982–1999 | 93 | 18 | **5.17/yr** |
| 2000–2009 | 38 | 10 | **3.80/yr** |
| 2010–2019 | 23 | 10 | **2.30/yr** |
| 2020–2025 | 6 | 6 | **1.00/yr** |
| **Full record (1982–2025)** | **160** | **44** | **3.64/yr** |

The 1990s were the most prolific decade (~5.9/yr). Six years had **zero** High Risk days: 2000, 2015, 2016, 2018, 2020, and 2022. Peak years were 1990 and 1991 (8 each).

---

### Monthly Distribution

| Month | Count | % of Total |
|---|---|---|
| January | 3 | 1.9% |
| February | 3 | 1.9% |
| **March** | **24** | **15.0%** |
| **April** | **42** | **26.2%** |
| **May** | **42** | **26.2%** |
| June | 20 | 12.5% |
| July | 7 | 4.4% |
| August | 1 | 0.6% |
| September | 1 | 0.6% |
| October | 5 | 3.1% |
| November | 9 | 5.6% |
| December | 3 | 1.9% |

**April and May are tied as the most prolific months**, each accounting for 26.2% of all High Risk days. Spring (March–May) accounts for **67.5%** of all High Risk days.

---

### May 17–30 Window: Deep Dive

**18 High Risk days** have fallen in the May 17–30 window across the 44-year record:

| Date | Notable Details |
|---|---|
| May 17, 1989 | LA/TX; 19 tornadoes, F3 max |
| May 24, 1989 | Midwest; 31 tornadoes, F4 max |
| May 20, 1990 | AR/KS/OK; 10 tornadoes |
| May 29, 1990 | KS/OK/TX; damaging wind threat |
| May 17, 1995 | OK; part of May 6–27 outbreak sequence |
| May 30, 1985 | Midwest; 17 tornadoes |
| May 28, 1987 | TX; 7 tornadoes |
| May 22, 2004 | NE/IA; 68 tornadoes, F4 (Hallam tornado) |
| May 24, 2004 | NE/IA; 54 tornadoes + derecho |
| May 29, 2004 | Central US; 80 tornadoes, F4 |
| May 30, 2004 | Central US; 86 tornadoes |
| May 22, 2008 | KS; 28 tornadoes |
| May 29, 2008 | NE/IA/SD; 37 tornadoes |
| May 19, 2010 | OK; 13 tornadoes (event busted) |
| May 24, 2011 | OK/KS/TX; 47 tornadoes, **EF5** |
| May 25, 2011 | Midwest; 94 tornadoes |
| May 18, 2017 | KS/OK; 58 tornadoes |
| May 20, 2019 | OK/TX; 51 tornadoes |

**Key statistics for May 17–30:**
- **18 of 160** total High Risk days (11.3%) fall in this 14-day window
- **18 of 42** May High Risk days (42.9%) fall in the back half of the month
- **11 of 44 years** (25%) had at least one High Risk day during May 17–30
- A day in this window is **~2.9× more likely** to be High Risk than a random day of the year
- Historical rate for any specific day in the May 17–30 window: **~2.9%** vs ~1.0% base rate

---

### Additional Notable Facts

- The most deadly High Risk day: **April 27, 2011** (316 fatalities, 215 tornadoes, 4 EF5s)
- Back-to-back High Risk days have not occurred since **April 27–28, 2014**
- Only 3 consecutive High Risk days ever recorded: **May 3–5, 1999** (Oklahoma outbreak)
- The longest gap between High Risk days: **964 days** (June 3, 2014 → January 22, 2017)
- Day 2 High Risk has only been issued 3 times in history
- The most recent High Risk day prior to this research: **April 2, 2025**

key_facts: SPC has issued 160 High Risk Day 1 Convective Outlooks across 44 years (1982-2025), averaging 3.64 per year over the full record.
Frequency has declined sharply: 5.17/yr in 1982-1999, 3.80/yr in 2000-2009, 2.30/yr in 2010-2019, and just 1.00/yr in 2020-2025.
April and May are tied as the most prolific months, each with 42 High Risk days (26.2% of all). Spring (Mar-May) accounts for 67.5% of all High Risk days.
18 High Risk days (11.3% of total) have fallen in the May 17-30 window, making days in that window ~2.9x more likely to be High Risk than a random day of the year.
11 of 44 years (25%) had at least one High Risk day during May 17-30.
42.9% of all May High Risk days (18 of 42) fall in the May 17-30 back half of the month.
Six years had zero High Risk days: 2000, 2015, 2016, 2018, 2020, 2022. Peak years were 1990 and 1991 with 8 each.
The most recent High Risk day was April 2, 2025. The most deadly was April 27, 2011 (316 fatalities, 215 tornadoes, 4 EF5s).
May 19 specifically has been a High Risk day once: May 19, 2010 (Oklahoma, though the event largely busted).
The longest gap between any two High Risk days was 964 days (June 3, 2014 to January 22, 2017).
